name: Embalse de El Villar via the Río Lozoya – circular from Buitrago de Lozoya
name_original: Embalse de El Villar por el Río Lozoya – circular desde Buitrago de Lozoya
region_intro: Hiking around Buitrago Del Lozoya offers access to diverse natural landscapes, situated between the Sierra del Guadarrama National Park and the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve. The region is characterized by the Lozoya River, which meanders around the historic town, and several reservoirs including Riosequillo and Puentes Viejas. Terrain includes dense forests, such as the Gariñas Forest, and more open, rugged areas, providing varied elevation and surface types for hikers. Best hiking trails around Buitrago Del Lozoya The most popular hiking route is Embalse de El Villar via the Río Lozoya – circular from Buitrago de Lozoya, an 18.0 miles (29.0 km) trail that takes 7 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Lozoya River and the El Villar reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Buitrago del Lozoya Wall – Buitrago de Lozoya loop from Buitrago del Lozoya, a moderate 7.1 miles (11.5 km) path. This route offers views of the medieval walls and the surrounding river landscape. Local hikers also love the Gariñas Forest – Lozoya River loop from Buitrago del Lozoya, an 8.7 miles (14.0 km) trail leading through the Gariñas Forest and along the Lozoya River, often completed in about 3 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around Buitrago Del Lozoya is defined by riverside paths, forest trails, and routes around reservoirs. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding routes. The routes in Buitrago Del Lozoya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1300 hikers have used komoot to explore Buitrago Del Lozoya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Buitrago del Lozoya?
A: Buitrago del Lozoya offers a network of over 10 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. These trails explore the diverse landscapes of the region, including riverside paths, forests, and reservoir surroundings.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners in Buitrago del Lozoya?
A: Yes, there are several easy hikes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed stroll. The region features gentle paths along the Lozoya River and through less challenging forest areas, providing accessible options for all.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Buitrago del Lozoya?
A: The terrain around Buitrago del Lozoya is quite varied. You can expect riverside paths, trails through dense forests like the Gariñas Forest, and routes around reservoirs. Some areas offer more open, rugged terrain with moderate elevation changes, especially as you approach the Sierra del Guadarrama foothills.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in the area?
A: For experienced trekkers, the region offers more demanding routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957499180/embalse-de-el-villar-via-the-rio-lozoya-circular-from-buitrago-de-lozoya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de El Villar via the Río Lozoya – circular from Buitrago de Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 29 km trail exploring the Lozoya River and the El Villar reservoir, taking nearly 8 hours to complete.

Q: Are there many circular hiking routes in Buitrago del Lozoya?
A: Yes, Buitrago del Lozoya is known for its circular routes, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40357098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gariñas Forest – Lozoya River loop from Buitrago del Lozoya&lt;/a&gt;, are designed as loops, offering a comprehensive exploration of the area's natural beauty.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking?
A: Hiking around Buitrago del Lozoya offers views of significant natural features. You can explore the Lozoya River, which meanders around the historic town, and several reservoirs like Riosequillo and Puentes Viejas. The Gariñas Forest provides beautiful woodland scenery. Notable landmarks include the medieval walls of Buitrago del Lozoya and natural highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find trails that lead to waterfalls in the area?
A: Yes, the region around Buitrago del Lozoya features natural attractions including waterfalls. You can find trails that lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de San Mamés&lt;/a&gt;, offering a refreshing natural spectacle, especially after rainfall.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or natural pools near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the Riosequillo reservoir, located near Buitrago, is home to the largest natural swimming pool in Spain. While not directly on every hiking route, it's a prominent feature in the area and offers a refreshing, chlorine-free swimming experience during warmer months, making it a great spot to cool off after a hike.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Buitrago del Lozoya suitable for families with children?
A: Many trails in Buitrago del Lozoya are suitable for families. The region offers easy walks with gentle gradients and interesting sights, such as the medieval walls and riverside paths, making them enjoyable for all ages. The Circular Trail of Buitrago del Lozoya is highlighted as suitable for all hiking levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Buitrago del Lozoya?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Buitrago del Lozoya area. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected natural areas like parts of the Sierra del Guadarrama National Park or the Sierra del Rincón Biosphere Reserve, and to check specific trail regulations beforehand.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads?
A: Buitrago del Lozoya is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services from Madrid. These services can get you to the town, which serves as a starting point for many trails. For specific trailheads further afield, local transport options might be limited, so planning is recommended.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Buitrago del Lozoya?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 200 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical charm of the medieval town, and the variety of trails that cater to different abilities, from gentle riverside walks to more challenging routes through forests and around reservoirs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Buitrago del Lozoya?
A: The best time to hike in Buitrago del Lozoya is typically during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring and colorful foliage in autumn. Summer can be hot, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience with cooler temperatures.
tips: This dam offers spectacular views. It is worth going there. Ideal for hiking, motorbike or road bike excursions. Next to the dam there is a small parking lot for about 10 cars.

The Lozoya is a river on the Iberian Peninsula, a tributary of the Jarama, which crosses the north of the Community of Madrid.
Buitrago del Lozoya is a Spanish municipality and town in the north of the Community of Madrid, located at the foot of the Sierra de Guadarrama. It gets its name from the Lozoya river, which surrounds the town.

The Lozoya is a river in the Iberian Peninsula, a tributary of the Jarama, which crosses the north of the Community of Madrid.
Buitrago del Lozoya is a Spanish municipality and town in the north of the Community of Madrid, located at the foot of the Sierra de Guadarrama. It receives its name from the Lozoya River, which surrounds the town.

The Buitrago del Lozoya wall is located in the homonymous municipality, in the northern part of the Community of Madrid. It is the best preserved walled enclosure in the Madrid region and the only one that remains in complete condition.

The impressive wall of Buitrago del Lozoya is located in the municipality of the same name, in the northern part of the Community of Madrid. It is the best preserved walled enclosure in the Madrid region and the only one that remains in a complete state.
